task_completion.description: The properties window for the target track/route is opened in Garmin BaseCamp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A properties dialog box for the imported item (which may originally be named 'fells_loop' or 'BELLEVUE') is visible on top of the main Garmin BaseCamp window.

task_completion.description: The track/route name is updated to the specified new name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Name field at the top of the properties window or the item name in the BaseCamp library list pane reads exactly 'Fells Reservation Survey - Fall 2024'.

task_completion.description: The display color of the track/route is changed to Red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Color dropdown menu in the properties window displays 'Red' or shows a red color block selected.

task_completion.description: The Notes/Description tab is accessed within the properties window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Notes' or 'Description' tab is actively selected in the properties dialog box, revealing a large text area.

task_completion.description: The Notes/Description field is updated with the specified survey text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text box in the Notes/Description tab contains exactly: 'Autumn wildlife corridor survey through Middlesex Fells Reservation. Route follows primary ridge trail for annual raptor census.'

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Garmin BaseCamp graphical user interface to make the edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Garmin BaseCamp application interface being used to edit fields, rather than a text editor (like Notepad, vim, or gedit) modifying the raw .gpx XML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands like 'sed', 'awk', or 'echo' being used to directly inject the new name, color, or description into the fells_loop.gpx file.

integrity.description: The application used is genuinely Garmin BaseCamp.
integrity.visual_evidence: The window titles, layout, map view, and library panes match the standard Garmin BaseCamp software interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Track Name: 'Fells Reservation Survey - Fall 2024'. Expected Color: 'Red'. Expected Description: 'Autumn wildlife corridor survey through Middlesex Fells Reservation. Route follows primary ridge trail for annual raptor census.' Note: The original fells_loop.gpx file actually contains route data (<rte> tags) named 'BELLEVUE' rather than track data (<trk> tags). The agent should be editing the properties of this imported route/item in Garmin BaseCamp to fulfill the task.
